frameworks.name: neuromodulatory systems (catecholamine vs acetylcholine)
frameworks.one_line: Distinct neuromodulator roles tested at cortex-wide network level in humans

frameworks.name: cortical circuit modeling (E/I balance and intracortical transmission)
frameworks.one_line: Circuit model linking pharmacology to local E/I balance and transmission changes

frameworks.name: exploration-exploitation in decision-making
frameworks.one_line: Catecholamine mechanism linked to increased behavioral exploration

core_question: Do catecholamines and acetylcholine produce dissociable effects on cortex-wide network interactions and behavior in humans?
key_assumptions: Pharmacological boosts selectively elevate target neuromodulator tone
Cortex-wide correlations index large-scale network interactions
Local E/I balance and intracortical transmission can be inferred from circuit models
manipulations: Pharmacological boost of catecholamine vs acetylcholine levels
Brain state: visual task vs rest
Behavioral task: perceptual choice vs value-based choice
key_findings: Catecholamine boost increased cortex-wide interactions during task but not rest
Acetylcholine boost decreased cortex-wide correlations during rest but not task
Circuit modeling attributed dissociation to differential E/I balance vs transmission changes
Catecholaminergic mechanism predicted and produced increased behavioral exploration
interpretations: Catecholamines and acetylcholine exert doubly dissociable effects on large-scale cortical dynamics
Differential modulation of local E/I balance vs intracortical transmission explains the dissociation
